characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Adaptation Name Change: They’re called the "Color Crew" in the short-lived 1996 reboot, and the "Color Guard" in the 2018 comic series. Age Lift: Their ages have flipped between being little kids to teenagers. Ambiguously Human: Besides Rainbow Brite, it's ambiguous if the Color Kids are actually human. Nothing is known of their backstories, they're abnormally short, they have unnatural hair colors, it's never mentioned if their names are nicknames or not, and it's implied they're older than they seem. Color-Coded for Your Convenience: Despite being named after their specific colors, all 7 (and later 8) color kids' outfits embody the color they represent. Demoted to Extra: Apart from Rainbow Brite and Red Butler, most of the Color Kids were reduced to this in the 2014 reboot. The 2018 comics don’t even have a majority of them appear aside from Red and Patty, but this was due to the comic beingCut Shortbefore they could debut.

characters.name: Rainbow Brite
characters.description: Although she maybe small, her good deeds and the magic she performs with her powerful Color Belt, more than make up for her size! Although full of fun, Rainbow always makes sure her work comes first. She may be enjoying a merry game with her friends, but should a sun-drenched field of wheat be in danger of losing its beautiful yellow color, or a forest of green trees need its green leaves reviving, Rainbow is there in a thrice providing them with all the color they need. She does this by scattering lots of Star Sprinkles which turn magical in Rainbow's fingers. Rainbow lives in the Color Castle with her friends. As a young girl called Wisp, she was chosen by the Sphere of Light to restore Rainbow Land from the reign of the King of Shadows, who wiped all color and light from the realm. Although she isn't one to back down from any challenge, Rainbow Brite takes on foes and friends alike as her main goal is to keep Rainbowland full of colors! Artistic Age: She could be anywhere from 6 to 12. Although her design varies over different continuities, her age is never actually confirmed/specified. Likewise, to appeal to a mass audience, her design takes cues from different ages, such as being short and chubby with cute hair, but also wearing a uniform of sorts and having mature opinions. The Chosen One: Apparently a combination of her own choice and that of the Sphere of Light. Dialogue indicates that as Wisp she wanted a place which needed her, and the Sphere of Light chose her to save Rainbow Land, even giving her the opportunity to back out once she saw the terrible state it was in. Conveniently an Orphan: If she hadn't been an orphan she wouldn't have been able to save Rainbow Land.

characters.name: Red Butler
characters.description: Red Butler made his first appearance in "Peril in the Pits", along with many of the other original characters. Red is an adventurous boy who is in charge of the red Star Sprinkles. He is often assisting the color kids and protecting Rainbow Land from Murky and Lurky. In typical hero fashion, with cape and a take charge attitude, his bravery can have its drawbacks as it often gets him into trouble. No fear, however, because the Color Kids and Sprites are always on hand to help and get him out of it, proving that every hero needs a helping hand. Red is usually seen supervising and taking charge of difficult tasks around Rainbow Land, such as mining the color caves for crystals, to supervising their cut into Star Sprinkles. Adaptational Hairstyle Change: His original incarnation had messy short hair, while his 2018 incarnation has spiky hair stylized to look like a fire. Adaptation Dye-Job: His 2018 incarnation has black hair, while previous incarnations were redheads.

characters.name: Lala Orange
characters.description: Lala Orange is the little girl in charge of all things orange. She is very lady-like and romantic. Her biggest crush is Red Butler, and they are often seen together. Lala's name is French, and she wears a beret, which is often associated with French fashion. Adaptational Jerkass: Her 2014 incarnation is apparently prone to butting heads with Rainbow Brite, and has a tendency to be abrasive due to her perfectionism. The Fashionista: She is quite into her appearance. Onion Johnny: She wears a beret as she is French. The Perfectionist: Her 2014 incarnation "wants everything and everyone to be perfectly fabulous".

characters.name: Canary Yellow
characters.description: Rainbow Brite's favorite Color Kid is Canary, because yellow is such a cheerful color! Canary always looks on the bright side, no matter how bad things seem, and she helps everyone to cheer up and smile again. Because she is so trusting, poor Canary Yellow is often caught by Murky Dismal, but Rainbow always rescues her. Canary Yellow's sprites work in the mines of the Yellow Plains to provide all the Star Sprinkles Rainbow Brite uses to make the yellow color needed in the world. Cheerful Child: She's the most perky of the Color Kids. Circus Brat: Dresses in an acrobat's leotard and is known for her gymnastics. Cloudcuckoolander: Her 2014 incarnation is stated to be a bit “out there”, and she's described as the"Andy Dwyer" to Rainbow's "Leslie Knope". Genki Girl: Although she's not as excitable as other examples, compared to the other girls, she's a cheerful ball of energy, though it'sDepending on the Writer.

characters.name: Patty O'Green
characters.description: Patty is very mischievous. She just loves playing jokes on everyone! But she has such a sweet nature that the others naturally forgive her. She is very proud of being in charge of the Green Grange section and does tend to boast a little. But when you think just how hard she and her sprites work, producing all the green needed in the world, well, she has every reason to be proud, doesn't she? Think how many leaves and blades of grass there are! Adaptational Nice Girl: Her 2018 incarnation is entirely nice and caring, showing no signs of being a mischievous prankster. Adaptation Name Change:Downplayed. Her name in the 2018 comics is still "Patty" (though minus the "O'Green" part), but it is merely a nickname that she uses, since her actual name is unpronounceable. Adaptation Species Change: She’s always been depicted as human-looking, but her 2018 comic incarnation is a dryad-like alien. The Baby of the Bunch: Her childish appearance, stemming from her bandaged legs, braided twin tails, bloomers, rounded face and generally younger attitude has her come across as this. Not to mention the fact that she is consistently chided by the other color kids and Rainbow for acting out and her immaturity.

characters.name: Buddy Blue
characters.description: Buddy and his blue sprites spend their days in the Blue Zone making sure there are enough blue Star Sprinkles for the blue sky, the waves of the sea and the blue flowers and all the blue things in the world. But Buddy is also a great athlete. He loves health food, and he talks about it non-stop. He often organizes team events and other sports programs and sporting activities. The others do get a bit bored with all this, but they try not to show it as Buddy is a very lovable boy. Academic Athlete: He is the most athletic character and often talks about healthy eating. His 2014 incarnation is described as “Athletic and smart”. Amusing Injuries: His 2014 incarnation holds the record for “most extreme skateboard kick-flip”, and the most amount of bones broken while doing it. Blue Is Heroic: Buddy is friendly and embodies the color blue.

characters.name: Indigo
characters.description: Indigo's ambition is to be a famous actress, and she spends her life 'on stage', rehearsing and reciting lines from famous plays, and wearing very imaginative costumes! She and her sprites in the Indigo Acres are rather dramatic and given to dreaming, and Indigo sees herself as a great artist full of drama and emotion. But she does make sure that there is a good supply of indigo Star Sprinkles. All There in the Manual: Her last name, “Doll”, is only referenced in a coloring book, which would make her “Indigo Doll”. Otherwise, she has only ever been referred to as just “Indigo”. Depending on the Artist: Due to indigo being between blue and violet, Indigo's color scheme tends to switch between being blue or purple in tone. Drama Queen: Her 2014 incarnation exaggerates her theatric personality into full-blown melodrama, to the point where even something as small as an untied shoelace can be considered the "BIGGEST. CRISIS. EVER." Large Ham: She's very into putting on a good performance, on and off the stage. So much so, that she wrote an entire play and only gave herself lines.

characters.name: Shy Violet
characters.description: The Star Sprinkles which Shy Violet and her Sprites produce in the Violet Valley are the color of wisdom and knowledge Violet is a shy genius who spends most of her time reading and writing all about color. She can help Rainbow Brite solve almost any problem, however difficult, and everyone asks her advice because she is always right! Full-Name Basis: When not referred to as "Violet" she is called by her full name. Hero of Another Story: Her 2014 incarnation is this in private, having a secret crime-fighting alter-ego called "The Purple Protector". Hidden Depths: Her 2014 incarnation is apparently a secret adrenaline junkie, and uses her equally secret superhero identity as an outlet for this. Meaningful Name: She is shy and she has violet hair.

characters.name: Moonglow
characters.description: Casting a Shadow: Her powers deal consistently with nighttime. Inconsistent Spelling: One VHS wrote her name as "Moonglo". Invisibility: She involuntarily turns invisible when she's working hard enough. Logical Weakness: Since her powers involve controlling the night, bright light makes it difficult for her to use them, and daytime sunlight is mentioned as nullifying her power completely. Too much exposure to unnaturally bright light could supposedly even burn out her powers for good. Mystical White Hair: Originally she was intended to have white hair but this was changed to pink because it was thought she looked old.

characters.name: Stormy
characters.description: Stormy is one of the residents of Rainbow Land. As her name implies, she has command of the storms and was in an altercation with Rainbow Brite when she didn't want to change the season from winter to spring. Amazing Technicolor Wildlife: Her horse is dark purple with a light purple mane. Dark Is Not Evil: Stormy has the darkest color palette out of the heroes. She is moody and sometimes mean but not an antagonist. Subverted in the 2014 version, where she starts off as one. Green-Eyed Monster: She indicates in the movie that, despite how much she loves making bad weather, she wishes she could make things bright and colourful like the other kids. Hellish Horse:Subverted. Skydancer looks the part but is not bad.

characters.name: Tickled Pink
characters.description: Adaptation Distillation: In her most basic form (her card appearances and movie debut) she too has an important cheek tattoo like Rainbow. However, come her TV and later appearances, it's gone without explanation. Although hers is meant to be just as significant (because she's also originally a human), it is waved off (some say as a temporary tattoo) and never reappears, and it isn't until the 2009 reboot does she gain prominence among Rainbow again. Dub Name Change: In Germany, she is known as Penny Popperpink. The Kirk: She's anice girland is the balance between Moonglow and Stormy's wildly different personalities.

characters.name: The Color Crew
characters.description: The group of friends filling in for the Color Kids in the franchise's second generation from 1996. It's members consisted of Rainbow Brite, Amber, Indigo, Cerise, Ebony, and Sparkle Brite. Adaptational Mundanity: Rather than magical beings from a different world who spread color across the universe, the Color Crew is a group of normal girls from Earth. Adapted Out: Everybody but Rainbow Brite and Indigo is replaced with new characters in this toy line. Anthropomorphic Personification: Instead of being a human child who ascended to who she is now, this Rainbow Brite is the child of the humanoid sun and the rainbow. Bioluminescence Is Cool: The Color Crew, instead of using Star Sprinkles to bring color to the Earth, uses glow-in-the-dark paint. The dolls came with tiny containers of washable glowing paint, plus paintbrushes, so you could paint on their clothes.

characters.name: The Sprites
characters.description: Cartoon Creature: They are very small, mothlike creatures with fuzzy antennae and round, large eyes. Color-Coded for Your Convenience: Every sprite has a different color, as well as pun based name. Meaningful Name: If their name isn't punny, then it probably serves some deeper meaning in connection to its character. Patchwork Kids: In the 2018 comics, it's mentioned that two sprites of different colors will produce a child who's color is a mix of their parents' colors. Punny Name: Like their owners, the color kids, their names are derived from color-based puns. See other installments of this trope for details.

characters.name: Starlite
characters.description: Amazing Technicolor Wildlife: His fur is white but his hair is rainbow-colored. Catchphrase: He'll take any chance he gets to claim he is the "most magnificent horse in the universe". Cool Horse: He certainly believes so and constantly brags about it. He can fly and talk. Insistent Terminology: His 2014 incarnation is a "verbose stallion",nota "talking horse". Jerk with a Heart of Gold: Not necessarily a "jerk" but very pompous, self-absorbed, and a braggart. But he'll endanger himself without a second thought to help Rainbow Brite.

characters.name: Puppy Brite and Kitty Brite
characters.description: Adaptation Name Change: In the 2009 reboot, Puppy Brite is renamed to “P.B.” Cute Kitten: Kitty Brite is an adorable little cat. Gender Flip: In the 2009 reboot, P.B. is a girl, when she was a boy in the 1980s cartoon. I Want My Beloved to Be Happy: Puppy Brite was the first friend Lurky made, but he gave Puppy over to the Color Kids, knowing he would be much happier in Rainbow Land than The Pits. Out of Focus: Compared to Puppy Brite, Kitty Brite has much less focus.

characters.name: Brian
characters.description: Adaptational Protagonist: In the 1980s series, he was a prominent character, but he only appeared in a third of the episodesnote"Peril in the Pits", "The Mighty Monstromurk Menace" parts 1 and 2, "Rainbow Night", and "Murky's Comet". In the 2014 reboot, he's theDeuteragonistand even gets more focus than the Color Kids. Adaptation Species Change: He's fully human in the 1980s series. In the 2014 reboot, he is half-Spectran, being the son of Krys. Awkward Relatable Schoolkid: Brian is an average boy from Earth who ends up getting roped into Rainbow Brite's adventures, with his normal nature serving as a contrast to the fantastical Rainbow Land. He's friendly and tries to help whenever he can. His 2014 reboot incarnation lampshaded his status as an everyboy:Brian:I don't know, Rainbow. I'm just a kid from Earth, who eats lunch every day alone in the library. Composite Character: Basically takes Krys' role as an armored hero who helps Rainbow Brite in the 2014 reboot. The Everyman: Is a bit of anAudience Surrogate, particularly for any boys that may or may not be in the audience.

characters.name: Krys
characters.description: Affirmative Action Girl:Gender Inverted. He's introduced as someone with a tenuous relation to the movie'sQuest Giverand presumably to give Rainbow a male costar in the hopes of attracting some interest from little boys. Age Lift: In the 2014 reboot, he became an adult since he was now Brian's father. Cool Horse: His mechanical steed is named On-X. Decomposite Character: In the 2014 series, Brian has Krys' powers and suit, having inherited his abilities. Foil: To Wisp, as while they're both competent kid warriors of their respective planets, Wisp is a leader while Krys is a definite soldier. This understandably fuels hiscomplex against Wisp, as he's shown to lack confidence and revert to petty gender stereotypes when Wisp outperforms him.

characters.name: Willow
characters.description: Ambiguous Situation: In the final issue of the 2018 comics, Willow is struck by lightning summoned by Gloomy, which indicates that she is a new incarnation of Stormy. However, since the series ended afterward, this was never confirmed. Canon Character All Along: If she was meant to be a new incarnation of Stormy, but otherwise she is ostensibly aCanon Foreigner.

characters.name: The King of Shadows
characters.description: Big Bad: For the 2-part TV special "The Beginning of Rainbow Land", and the 2018 comic book series. Casting a Shadow: He has powers over darkness. Dark Is Evil: He has shadow powers, and is evil. Empty Piles of Clothing: The King of Shadows vanishes with just his cloak behind when Rainbow Brite puts an end to his reign of terror. Glowing Eyes of Doom: The King of Shadows only has his glowing eyes and hands visible. Oh, and the former glow yellow, which also makes himYellow Eyes of Sneakiness, except in the SNES game, though, where his eyes are white.

characters.name: Murky Dismal
characters.description: Adaptational Hairstyle Change: He was made bald in the 2014 reboot, and only a ponytail on top of his head in the 2018 comics. Adaptational Slimness: He went from being overweight in his 1980s incarnation, to a diminutive short and skinny man in 2014, and then became a regular skinny man in 2018. Bald of Evil: His 2014 incarnation. Downplayed with his 2018 incarnation, who is mostly bald aside from his ponytail. Big Bad: He's the recurring enemy of the Color Kids. Dragon Ascendant: Was originally a minion of the King of Shadows. After the King's demise, he became the main villain himself.

characters.name: Lurky
characters.description: Lurky is Murky’s partner in crime, created one day to serve as his servant. Whereas Murky is clever and cunning, Lurky is strong but very dim witted. Still, Murky tolerates him as best he can, though he frequently insults the big lug. Adaptational Curves: His 2014 incarnation is incredibly muscular and buff, a far cry from his fatter 1980s and 2018 incarnations. Affably Evil: He's pretty nice and can hardly even be called evil. The Ditz: He's pretty dim and silly. Dumb Muscle: He's big and he's not the smartest character by far.

characters.name: The Dark Princess
characters.description: The main villain ofthe movie, she is a spoiled princess with dark powers who seeks to steal the diamond planet Spectra, even though taking it would drain all color and life from the universe and eventually destroy it. Though her plan failed and her spaceship exploded, she returned in the final TV episode to menace Rainbow Land, enslaving the Sprites to dig up diamonds in an attempt to regain her lost powers. She is also the main villain of every reboot of the series. Adaptational Attractiveness: Her 2014 incarnation looks less like a crazed madwoman compared to her original 1980s incarnation. Adapted Out: She's nowhere to be seen in the 2018 comics, while the other recurring villains made an appearance or at least got mentioned. Amulet of Concentrated Awesome: Her magic jewel. When it's destroyed, hercastleandarmycrumble into sand. And I Must Scream: She threatens to turn Rainbow Brite into a diamond to wear around her neck.

characters.name: Count Blogg
characters.description: Amazing Technicolor Population: He'sa green humanoidwithred eyes. Bodyguard Betrayal: Possibly. He personally oversaw the Princess's ship's construction and fueling. When it finally takes off, it seems there wasn't enough fuel to get far from the Princess's home planet at all. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Never reappeared after the 1980s series ended. Fake Wizardry: In the show, he uses trickery to pretend he turned Twink into a frog to force the Sprites to obey, when in fact Twink was just bound and gagged. Yes-Man: He serves the Princess's every whim. Doesn't do enough of the heavy lifting to even qualify asThe Dragon.

characters.name: Murky Dismal's Creations
characters.description: Robot Me: A near identical copy of Rainbow Brite. Wind-Up Key: It had one on the back of her head, but it would constantly power down because it couldn’t stay wound up for long. Genie in a Bottle: Evokes this imagery, but isn’t a genie himself. Sealed Evil in a Can: While it’s one of Murky's strongest creations, it’s too powerful for him to control, and so he sealed it in a magic bottle to contain it. Hyper-Competent Sidekick: He isn’t a major villain, but he ultimately ends up capturing Red Flare and Wisp with little effort. With the comic beingCut Shortbefore either could free themselves, it also meanshe singlehandedly defeated the remaining resistanceagainst the King of Shadows.

characters.name: Minions
characters.description: Mooks: They are large in number and serve as mere obstacles in the Color Kids' way. Dark Is Evil: Like their master, the Minions are shadowy figures who hate color. Green and Mean: All of them are green and incredibly aggressive.
